free_text: Activation cross sections of proton induced nuclear
reactions on gold up to 65 MeV
Natural gold foil, thickness: 25 microns as backing of
Ga-Ni (70-30%) electroplated samples
Stack1: 65MeV, 84nA, 1h, LVN, 18 Au targets, 25um
Stack2: 65MeV, 35nA, 1h, LVN, 17 Au targets,23.1um
Stack3: 33.7MeV, 90nA, 1h, VUB, 11 Au targets, 23.1um
The activities of the produced radionuclides were
measured nondestructively without chemical separation.
The energy degradation as a function of depth in the
stack was calculated from the input energy and target
thicknesses.
Chapter 4
Decay data were taken from NuDat2.6 database.
The uncertainty of the primary beam energy
was estimated as (+-0.2 MeV) which increased due to
straggling effect continuously in each foil through
the stack.
Total uncertainty is between 10-12%.
number of bombarding particles (7%)
surface density of target atoms (1%)
detector efficiency (5%)
beam loss correction (1.5%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-20%)
The experimental results were compared to the
result calculated by the ALICE-D, EMPIRE-D and TENDL
theoretical model codes.
Integral yields were calculated from the
fitted experimental cross sections.
Stack1 (2BLGLVN) 65 MeV
Stack2 (2BLGLVN) 65 MeV
Stack3 (2BLGVUB) 33.7 MeV
